items.readinessTheme: Accelerate flagship rollout after wrapper retirement
items.boardQuestion: Which AI lane is genuinely ready to scale into wider operating use right now?
items.currentPosture: The AI flagship has absorbed wrapper tools and memo reuse is climbing, but leadership still needs one tighter readiness brief before wider rollout.
items.requiredProof: Wrapper retirements, flagship reuse, and lower coordination drag across the AI lane.
items.headline: AI is now an accelerate-ready lane instead of a scattered wrapper story.
items.narrative: The strongest proof is that one flagship system is carrying more decision work while orchestration drag has fallen.
items.nextMove: Package flagship reuse and wrapper retirement evidence into one board-ready acceleration packet.

items.readinessTheme: Fortify owner readiness before wider consolidation
items.boardQuestion: Which lane is strategically important but not yet ready to scale because ownership still runs thin?
items.currentPosture: Identity is stable enough to protect, but remediation ownership and consolidation readiness are still uneven.
items.requiredProof: Owner coverage, remediation closure timing, and avoided drag from duplicated identity controls.
items.headline: Identity needs fortification before leadership treats it as a scaling lane.
items.narrative: The board story here is not scale yet; it is to strengthen owner readiness so future leverage does not weaken the control surface.
items.nextMove: Fortify remediation ownership and prove the lane can scale without adding new control drag.

items.readinessTheme: Hold acceleration until analyst time moves into cleaner reporting throughput
items.boardQuestion: Where can we free capacity and turn it into better reporting throughput instead of more dashboard churn?
items.currentPosture: Revenue systems have reduced backlog noise, but capacity still leaks into manual explanation work and tool overlap.
items.requiredProof: Removed reporting loops, faster answer cycles, and cleaner throughput from fewer reporting surfaces.
items.headline: Revenue should hold broad acceleration until throughput stays cleaner for longer.
items.narrative: The leverage story is strongest when the board can see analyst hours moving out of reconciliation work and into decision support.
items.nextMove: Hold wider acceleration until reporting capacity shifts into one cleaner answer path and throughput gains stick.

items.readinessTheme: Escalate drag clusters before promising scalable savings
items.boardQuestion: Which lane still has too much drag and too little reinvestment confidence to scale safely?
items.currentPosture: Payments, treasury, merchant review, and KYC all exist, but the operating stack still behaves like separate proof pockets rather than one leverage lane.
items.requiredProof: Shared ownership, reduced queue drag, and higher reinvestment confidence across the FinTech family.
items.headline: FinTech should be escalated before the board treats it as a scalable leverage story.
items.narrative: The right move is to escalate drag pockets now rather than turn fragmented proof into an overclaimed scale narrative.
items.nextMove: Escalate queue drag and ownership gaps into one diligence packet before approving wider leverage claims.

items.readinessTheme: Accelerate one answer core across the buyer path
items.boardQuestion: Where can one already-clean proof core scale across more buyer and diligence motions?
items.currentPosture: Procurement packaging is consolidated, trust evidence is reusable, and the answer core is ready to carry more volume.
items.requiredProof: Lower cycle time, reused answers, and fewer custom proof packets across buyer workflows.
items.headline: Procurement already has a scalable answer core that can carry more buyer load.
items.narrative: The leverage signal comes from one answer system being reused across procurement, trust, and RFP workflows with lower custom effort.
items.nextMove: Scale the answer core across procurement, trust center, and RFP lanes with one board-readable reuse packet.

items.readinessTheme: Fortify regulated throughput before wider operating reuse
items.boardQuestion: Which regulated lane could become leverage later but first needs stronger throughput proof without control erosion?
items.currentPosture: The biotech stack is stable and credible, but leadership still needs a clearer case that quality throughput is improving without weakening controls.
items.requiredProof: Maintained control quality, faster case movement, and no new audit drag across GxP, CAPA, specimen, and safety lanes.
items.headline: Biotech is a fortify-first lane: stronger throughput proof now, wider leverage later.
items.narrative: The board story is that control quality held while throughput improved, not that the regulated stack was stripped too aggressively.
items.nextMove: Fortify throughput evidence and publish a continuity packet before broader leverage claims are made.
